fix(cli): deduplicate handleSubmit when autocomplete Enter and TextInput onSubmit both fire

submittingRef guard blocks the second synchronous call.

commit.message:
fase 3: map + crawl analysis complete

Map de 4 sitios (17-1381 URLs) y crawl completo de Asconia (20 págs) y Ucanca (31 págs).
Hallazgo: map→scrape→decide crawl es el pipeline óptimo para lead research ICP.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Split gate/up FFN weights with fused SiLU, parallelize SSM + attention (~119ms/tok)

- Split combined gate_up weight [2*n_ff, n_embd] into separate gate [n_ff, n_embd]
  and up [n_ff, n_embd] tensors, each stored as BFP8 on device
- Gate matmul uses fused SiLU activation (activation="silu" in ttnn::matmul),
  eliminating 3 ops per layer (2 slices + 1 silu) from the traced FFN chain
- Fuse gated RMSNorm into SSM delta-net v-head loop, reducing memory passes
- Use stack-allocated accumulator in attention to avoid shared static buffer
- Result: 130ms→119ms/tok (~8.4 tok/s)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: budget ledger and actor quality signal — close the judgement loop

Budget Ledger (budget_ledger.py):
- Append-only JSONL at .workgraph/budget-ledger.jsonl tracking
  creates/dispatches per actor with timestamps
- recent_count() and recent_count_by_class() for real hourly rate limiting
- Wired into guarded_add_drift_task_with_authority() — max_creates_per_hour
  is now actually enforced (was previously always 0)

Actor Quality Signal (quality_signal.py):
- Per-actor quality score (0.0-1.0) computed from drift outcome history
- Formula: resolved_rate - 2*worsened_rate - 0.5*ignored_rate
- Trend detection: compares last 7 days vs previous 23 days
- quality_budget_modifier() returns 0.5x-1.5x multiplier for budgets
- format_quality_briefing() produces agent-readable feedback

Feedback Loop Closure:
- DriftOutcome gains actor_id field (backward-compatible)
- task-completing.sh passes actor_id when recording outcomes
- task-claimed.sh injects quality briefing at task start
- CLI: driftdriver quality briefing/scores/all
- CLI: outcome-from-check gains --actor-id flag

This closes the two blocking gaps for autonomous operation:
real budget enforcement and outcome-to-agent feedback.

1597 tests passing.

commit.message:
Add embedding-based dedup for propose-beliefs

Uses fastembed (BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5) to score existing beliefs by
cosine similarity to batch content, replacing keyword overlap for
selecting which beliefs to include in the dedup prompt. Belief vectors
are cached in .code-expert/belief-vectors.json (keyed by text hash,
auto-invalidated on change). First embed of 280 beliefs takes ~6s,
cached loads are instant (~0.02s).

fastembed is an optional dependency — falls back to keyword overlap
scoring when not installed. Install with: pip install 'code-expert[embeddings]'

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add advisory lock to prevent deadlocks between enrichment and cost basis

Both enrichment (bulkUpdateTransactions) and cost basis (recomputeCostBasis)
do bulk UPDATEs on the transactions table. Running them concurrently caused
Postgres deadlocks. Use pg_advisory_lock with a shared lock ID so one waits
for the other instead of deadlocking.

repository.name: config

commit.message:
fix: add x-systemd.mount-timeout=5s to all CIFS automounts

COSMIC Files stat()s sidebar favorites on startup, triggering automount
for each network share. Without a mount timeout, systemd waits 90s per
share before giving up, causing COSMIC Files to hang when servers are
offline. 5s timeout keeps it snappy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

and version bump to 0.1.1.

foundation layer (TODO-007a, Day 1) for panel.zyntapos.com.

Includes:
- Vite 6 + React 19 + TypeScript 5 (strict mode) project config

- All 9 TypeScript domain interfaces (license, store, user, audit,
  sync, metrics, config, alert, api pagination)
- Utility functions: formatCurrency(LKR), maskLicenseKey, formatBytes,
  buildQueryString, formatRelativeTime

- Vitest + RTL + MSW test infrastructure (80% coverage thresholds)

- nginx.conf: SPA fallback, gzip, security headers, 1y asset caching
- .gitignore, .nvmrc (Node 22), .env.example, .prettierrc, eslint.config.js

- Desktop (>=1024px): full sidebar (240px) + collapse toggle

- StatusBadge: color-coded for 17 status variants
- ToastContainer: auto-dismiss toasts, positioned above bottom nav on mobile
- LoadingState: Skeleton, CardSkeleton, TableSkeleton, PageLoader

All touch targets >=44px. Root layout wired to AppShell.

API:

All mobile-first responsive. Charts use ResponsiveContainer.

* chore: add VPS ad-hoc workflow for diagnostics

* fix(deploy): use /health endpoint for admin-panel healthcheck

nginx.conf exposes /health at port 3000; using the dedicated endpoint
avoids wget exit-code issues when fetching the SPA root.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013es7eQ6g88H4yhqUHzNyUw

* fix(deploy): use 127.0.0.1 in nginx container healthchecks

Alpine Linux resolves 'localhost' to ::1 (IPv6) but nginx only
listens on IPv4. Using 127.0.0.1 fixes healthcheck for:
  - canary (also use /ping endpoint instead of / for explicit 200)
  - admin-panel
  - website (use / instead of /ping which Astro doesn't serve)

https://claude.ai/code/session_013es7eQ6g88H4yhqUHzNyUw

* fix(ci): align admin-panel VITE env var names with React source

Build step and Docker build-args used VITE_API_BASE_URL / VITE_LICENSE_BASE_URL /
VITE_SYNC_BASE_URL but the React source and Dockerfile ARGs expect VITE_API_URL /
VITE_LICENSE_URL / VITE_SYNC_URL. Mismatched names caused the env vars to be silently
ignored during Docker image build, falling back to Dockerfile defaults.

Also corrects the build-args values to use production URLs (matching Dockerfile
defaults) for consistency.

causing the VPS to run without a valid GHCR image for the admin-panel
container. Added build-admin-panel-image job and included it in
trigger-deploy needs so the deploy only fires after all images are ready.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013es7eQ6g88H4yhqUHzNyUw

* docs(todo): add TODO-007f CF + Custom auth plan for admin panel

7-day implementation plan for replacing Cloudflare Access-only auth
(Option 1) with a hybrid CF + Custom approach:
- Cloudflare: DDoS, WAF, TLS (kept as-is, orange-cloud proxy)
- Custom: ZyntaPOS-branded login, backend JWT admin auth API,
  brute-force lockout, TOTP MFA, Google SSO (@zyntasolutions.com),
  admin user management UI, full auth audit trail

Plan covers:
- Day 1: Ktor admin auth routes + PostgreSQL schema (admin_users,
  admin_sessions, admin_mfa_backup_codes) + JWT issuance
- Day 2: React login UI + Zustand auth store (replaces CF cookie)
- Day 3: Security hardening (headers, CSRF, rate limiting, lockout)
- Day 4: TOTP MFA (setup wizard, QR code, backup codes)
- Day 5: Google OAuth 2.0 PKCE SSO (domain-restricted)
- Day 6: Admin user management CRUD UI
- Day 7: Tests, CF Access bypass migration, CI update

No paid auth services required. All self-hosted in existing Ktor backend.
Updated 000-execution-plan.md to include 007f in Phase 2 TODO table.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013es7eQ6g88H4yhqUHzNyUw

* docs(todo): update 007f roles — SUPER_ADMIN/OPERATOR/FINANCE/AUDITOR

Replace placeholder roles (SUPER_ADMIN/SUPPORT/VIEWER) with final
business-aligned 4-role design:

  SUPER_ADMIN — Company owner/CTO. Full access. MFA mandatory.
  OPERATOR    — Technical support engineers. Remote diagnostics,
                sync management, ops health. MFA mandatory.
  FINANCE     — Accountant/billing. Financial reports and license
                read-only. No operational access.
  AUDITOR     — Internal/external auditors. Read-only: audit logs
                + all reports. Zero write access by design.

Changes:
- Add Section 3.5: Role Definitions, Permission Matrix (full),
  granular permission constants (TypeScript + Kotlin), MFA policy
  per role, Google SSO auto-provisioning policy (→ AUDITOR)
- Update DB schema CHECK constraint to new role values
- Update JWT payload role comment
- Update MFA enforcement: SUPER_ADMIN + OPERATOR (was SUPER_ADMIN only)
- Update Day 5 SSO: auto-provision as AUDITOR (was VIEWER)
- Update Day 6 UI: role selector + validation checklist
- Update all validation checklists with role-specific access assertions

- SupportTicketRepository.kt — domain contract (getAll / getByStatus / assignedTo /
  getByCustomer / getByToken / insert / update / addActivity / generateTokenNumber / search)

on the first coordinator refresh after HA restart.

commit.message:
Shrink system prompt 600→80 tokens, fix Arabic bug

Problem: 600-token system prompt caused:
  1. 2.5-minute response times on low-end hardware
  2. 'NEVER Chinese, NEVER English' → model picked Arabic instead

Fix:
  - Rewrote system prompt in Hebrew (the target language) — 80 tokens
  - Language rule: 'ענה תמיד בעברית' (positive rule, no 'NEVER X')
  - Technical terms in English explicitly allowed
  - Kept only critical rules: reboot, localectl, no setxkbmap
  - MAX_HISTORY_TURNS: 4 → 3 (6 messages instead of 8)

Expected: response time for 'hi' drops from 2.5min to ~20-40 seconds

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add HTTPS support for ESP32 Railway backend connection

- Skip SSL certificate validation with setInsecure()
- Add redirect following for image downloads
- Enables connection to Railway HTTPS endpoint

feat: adapt logo upload to S3 pre-signed URL flow (#25)

* feat: adapt logo upload to S3 pre-signed URL flow (#24)

Replace direct multipart POST to /assets/logo with the two-step
pre-signed URL flow: request upload credentials from the API, then
POST the file directly to S3 using the returned uploadUrl and
uploadFields. Fetch a fresh downloadUrl via GET /assets/logo/{logoId}
after upload. Also adds 2 MB client-side file size validation and
bumps the SDK to 0.1.4.

commit.message:
Add Recipe Costing Phase 1 — costPerUnit field, recipe cost summary, costing page

Part 1 — Ingredients:
- Add optional Cost per Unit ($) field to add and edit forms
- Write costPerUnit to Firestore on add and edit
- Display $X.XX/unit under ingredient name in table when set

Part 2 — Recipes page:
- Compute batch cost and cost per unit inline in expanded recipe rows
- Show "Estimated Cost: $X.XX per batch · $X.XX per unit" when costs exist
- Show "Add ingredient costs to see costing" when none are set

Part 3 — New /costing page:
- One card per recipe with full ingredient cost breakdown table
- Columns: Ingredient | Qty | Unit | Cost/Unit | Line Cost
- Missing costs shown in amber with "No cost set"
- Card border green (all costs set) or amber (any missing)
- ingredientMap pattern for O(1) ingredient lookups across recipes

Navbar: add Costing to Business dropdown between Sales and Purchasing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

SW: force reload all open tabs when new SW activates to clear stale state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: replace h-dvh with percentage height chain to fix iOS PWA bottom gap (#567)

On iOS PWA, 100dvh is miscalculated on initial load (before WebKit
initializes safe areas fully) and only corrects after a resize/rotation event.
This causes a visible gap below the bottom navigation rail on first load.

Fix: propagate height via html/body/#root (all height: 100%) and use
flex-1 on the layout container instead of h-dvh. Percentage heights are
stable and don't suffer from the iOS dvh timing bug.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(relationships): CC humanization + warmth pass — recognition architecture, move diversity, stewardship

Rebuild the Relationship Command Center from administrative to human:

- AvatarCircle: state-colored rings (ringClassName prop), warmer monogram fallback
- PriorityPersonCard: full rebuild — lg avatar, recognition line ("{segment} · {context}"),
  always-visible recommended move, state-colored strength dots, card bg tint + hover glow
- NextMoveCard: contact photo with move-type badge overlay, per-move pill variants + icon colors
- Recognition line system: buildRecognitionLine() pure helper (segment + drift/state context)
- Move rebalancing: widened triggers for check_in, reconnect, schedule, congratulate
  to prevent collapse into reach_out/follow_up
- deriveRecommendedMove: all 8 states return non-null moves
- Signal cards: severity-colored icons (rose/amber/zinc by severity)
- Signal microcopy: contextual count-based variants for all 6 cards
- CommitmentsPanel: rewarding empty state (emerald check + sub-line), warmer subtitle
- Radar: state legend, richer tooltip (strength + commitments), initials on large nodes
- SignalsFeed: larger state dots, stronger hover
- HeroStrip: warmer dual-tone gradient (violet + indigo)
- MomentumBar: fill opacity 0.7 → 0.85
- Telemetry: ccMomentumExpand event

13 files changed. No new DB tables, API routes, or feature flags.
Build verified clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add AI player, knight piece shapes, and piece orientation fixes

- Single Player mode with greedy AI (easy/medium/hard difficulty)
- AI evaluates all moves by Euclidean distance to oasis f6, prioritises instant win
- 1-second AI thinking delay with pulsing HUD indicator
- Mode selection screen (Single Player vs Two Players) on game start
- Redesigned pieces as chess knight silhouettes (base, tapered column, collar, neck, head, muzzle)
- Knight heads dynamically rotate to face the board center as pieces move
- White pieces brightened to #ffffff; black pieces lightened to visible grey (#7a7a8a)
- Expanded piece starting positions (12 per side) and added vertical slide movement

commit.message:
Auto-generate daily preview article after 9am ET

Previously required ?generate=true param. Now auto-generates
on first request after 9am ET if no cached preview exists.
Cache TTL is 12 hours so it only generates once per day.

commit.message:
Sprint 5 complete — global analytics dashboard + per-session expansion

- Add /analytics page with:
  - Overview cards (sessions, questions, upvotes, answer rate, engagement score)
  - Questions-per-session bar chart (color-coded by answer rate)
  - Topic word cloud from cluster titles (sized by frequency)
  - AI insights generator (trend analysis, engagement gaps, recommendations)
  - Session comparison table (sortable, color-coded answer rate, live/ended status)
  - Unanswered questions attention report with top questions by upvotes
- Expand per-session analytics:
  - 6-metric grid (questions, upvotes, answered %, topics, replies, engagement score)
  - Average response time (time between question and first host reply)
  - Engagement gaps (popular clusters with no host response)
  - Link to global analytics
- Add Analytics link to sidebar

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
